score_note: Majorly swaps the cell/room/door cardinality conditions and repeats incorrect definitions.
Mentions minima and a cardinality bound, but misses the image characterization and gives a flawed explanation.
Partly identifies point insertion and color insertion, but garbles names and omits incidence counting.
Partly describes image/missing/typed missing color, but contains errors and misses the parity-fiber role.
Says room_of_colorful comes before picking a point, but misses nonempty and the cardinality argument.
Hallucinates unrelated near-critical/square counting and does not describe dbcountingset correctly.
Replaces the singleton outside-door argument with an unrelated graph handshaking argument.
Understands that odd parity implies existence, but misses the exact colorful-room count equation.
States the theorem incorrectly and misses colorful Nonempty, default, and Inhabited I.
Gives an iterative f-orbit proof instead of the Scarf/Fcolor/grid/room_point_seq construction.
Recognizes finite constant-subsequence extraction, but applies it to the wrong iterative sequence rather than constant C.
Mentions outside-C coordinates tending to zero, but misses coordinate inequalities and overstates the role.
Mentions compactness and vanishing diameter, but not the transfer-inequality role.
Mentions colorful rooms, limits, continuity, and coordinate inequality, but remains generic and omits colored witnesses.
Describes a different contradiction/cycle strategy and misses the actual Scarf-subsequence-diameter proof.
Partly explains deficit/tPush/pushTowardsZ and blockSum, but does not clearly state positive normalization.
Explains projection/embedding as a retraction, but misses the lifted map and projected fixed-point argument.
Hallucinates a Baire category proof instead of the Brouwer_Product construction.
Mentions product simplices and expected payoff, but misses mixedS/unilateral-deviation formal details.
Mentions payoff improvement, normalization, continuity, and fixed point, but incorrectly frames it as best-response/pure equilibrium.
Mostly restates game definitions and does not connect Brouwer.mixedGame, nash_map fixed point, pure deviations, or mixed_g_linear.
States two incident rooms and evenness, but gives an unrelated hypergraph account and omits fiber size.
Hallucinates a pigeonhole/color argument and misses the typed nearly colorful versus colorful partition.
Recognizes evenness for non-colorful typed nearly colorful rooms, but gives the wrong mechanism and misses the two-door fiber.
Mentions TT, finite grid, and TT.ILO, but misses division by l and drifts into generic fixed-point discussion.
Mischaracterizes size bounds as exhaustive finite search and misses dominance/diameter/outside-C roles.
Recognizes the need for a constant color pattern subsequence, but misses outside-C and in-C coordinate inequalities.
Gives a generic Brouwer explanation and misses the actual coordinate/sum/extensionality argument.
Explains flat-to-block indexing and inverse functions, but omits sum transformations.
Mentions blockWeight as card/total_card, but confuses it with pushTowardsZ and misses sum-one/project recovery.
Incorrectly claims the projection is not continuous in the product topology and misses Continuous.div/positive denominator.
Identifies the tPush case split and blockWeight formula, but the tPush = 0 reason is wrong and zero deficit is missing.
Correctly explains mixed_g_linear as linear expected payoff for mixed deviations as weighted pure outcomes.
Mentions reindex/map_simplex-style transport, but misses Fintype.equivFin/ProductSimplices/fixed-point pullback.
Mentions g_function nonnegativity and sum at least one, but does not clearly explain normalization to sum exactly one.
Mentions H1 sum_g > 1, fixed point, and contradiction, but the actual positive-coordinate/self-division mechanism is confused.
